name: Principal Bank
description_short: Principal Bank is an FDIC-insured online bank specializing in retirement accounts (IRAs) with savings products, money market accounts, and CDs.
description_long: Principal Bank is a subsidiary of Principal Financial Group, a major financial services company with a long history in retirement planning and insurance. The bank operates as an online-only institution focused specifically on Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and related savings products, leveraging Principal's broader ecosystem of retirement and investment solutions. This specialized approach distinguishes it from traditional full-service banks that offer checking, savings, and lending across all customer segments. Principal Bank's primary offerings include FDIC-insured traditional and Roth IRAs, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and savings accounts within IRA structures. All deposits are FDIC-insured up to $250,000 per depositor. The bank also manages automatic rollover IRAs for customers whose 401(k)s from former employers have been transferred out of employer plans. These products are designed as low-risk, interest-bearing alternatives positioned within the context of retirement planning rather than general banking. What distinguishes Principal Bank is its integration with Principal Financial's broader retirement ecosystem, including 401(k) plan administration, annuities, and financial planning services. Customers can coordinate IRA savings with employer retirement plans and other Principal products through a single platform. The company also offers estate planning, financial planning tools, and benefits navigation—services typically unavailable at online-only banks. The FDIC insurance on IRAs provides depositor protection, though this is less common than FDIC insurance on standard savings accounts. The main limitation is that Principal Bank does not appear to offer checking accounts, lending products, or traditional banking services—it is purely a savings and IRA-focused institution. There is no public information about interest rates, account minimums, or fees on the provided website content. Customers seeking comprehensive banking services would need to use another institution for checking, debit cards, or loans. The specialization in retirement accounts means it serves a narrower market than traditional banks.
services: FDIC-insured money market accounts within IRAs
FDIC-insured certificates of deposit (CDs) within IRAs
Automatic rollover IRA management for 401(k) transfers
pros: FDIC insurance on IRA deposits up to $250,000 per depositor, providing protection beyond typical IRA custodian coverage
Automatic rollover IRA management for 401(k)s from former employers, simplifying account consolidation
Integration with Principal's 401(k) plans, annuities, and retirement planning services within one platform
Online-only model eliminates branch overhead, potentially supporting competitive rates on savings and CDs
Access to Principal's financial planning tools, estate planning resources, and benefits counseling
Member FDIC status and Member SIPC designation (for securities offerings) provides regulatory oversight
Offers both traditional and Roth IRA options with FDIC-insured savings, money market, and CD products
cons: No checking accounts, debit cards, or payment services—purely a savings/IRA bank requiring external account for everyday banking
Website content does not disclose interest rates, minimum deposit requirements, or fee structures, making cost comparison difficult
Limited to IRA products and cannot serve as a primary bank for most consumers; narrow product focus excludes personal loans, credit cards, or mortgages
No information about customer service responsiveness, mobile app features, or ease of account opening and management
Automatic rollover IRAs may charge fees or have restrictions not detailed on the public website
best_for: Existing Principal Financial customers consolidating retirement accounts and seeking FDIC-insured IRA savings
Employees rolling over 401(k)s from former employers who want automatic rollover IRA management with FDIC protection
Conservative retirement savers prioritizing safety and FDIC insurance over higher returns from market-based investments
Individuals coordinating multiple retirement vehicles (401(k), IRA, annuity) who benefit from Principal's integrated platform
diagnosis: Principal Bank is best for retirement savers already invested in Principal's ecosystem who want FDIC-insured IRA products and integration with 401(k) plans or annuities. The main caveat is that it functions only as a specialized retirement savings bank—not a primary checking account or general banking provider—and requires customers to maintain separate accounts elsewhere for daily banking and spending needs.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
